title: bee herding enable/disable/status CLI verbs toggle the dispatch owner-enable marker
action: Add a 'herding' CLI verb group to bee.mjs: 'bee herding enable', 'bee herding disable', 'bee herding status' (per D1, D2). Create skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/herding.mjs exporting resolveHerdingMainRoot() that mirrors EXACTLY the git-common-dir resolution and ENABLE_BASENAME ('bee-herding.enable') constant read_first shows in .claude/skills/bee-herding/scripts/dispatch-interlock.mjs, plus enableHerding()/disableHerding()/herdingStatus() operating on <main-root>/.bee/tmp/bee-herding.enable. enable and disable are idempotent: enable never errors when the marker already exists, disable never errors when the marker is already absent (per D3). Wire three COMMAND_REGISTRY entries (herding.enable, herding.disable, herding.status; invoke 'bee herding enable' etc.; description states this creates/removes the SAME marker dispatch-interlock.mjs reads) next to the existing worktree group entries, three matching HANDLERS entries, and a herdingUsageFallback function added to GROUP_USAGE_FALLBACKS (mirror worktreeUsageFallback's shape exactly). herding.status emits {enabled, marker, main_root} in --json mode and a one-line text form otherwise, same shape as dispatch-interlock.mjs's own stdout. Per D5 (explicit user decision): add NO runtime guard — no TTY/interactivity check, no exclusion from the --help --json manifest, no confirmation prompt. Per D4: do not modify dispatch-interlock.mjs (it stays the sole reader) and do not wire the new verbs into any bee-herding bootstrap/dispatch/merge script or any other automation — grep skills/bee-herding/** and lib/** for any call site before capping, to prove nothing invokes it automatically; these verbs exist for a human to type directly. Write skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_herding_cli.mjs FIRST using the runModuleWorker + makeTempRepo harness test_cli_state.mjs already demonstrates for bee.mjs subprocess CLI tests; confirm it fails (RED) against today's bee.mjs before implementing, then implement lib/herding.mjs and the bee.mjs wiring until it passes (GREEN) — RED/GREEN discipline per bee-writing-skills' Iron Law.

truths: `bee herding status --json` on a repo with no marker reports {enabled:false, marker:<path>, main_root:<path>}
`bee herding enable` creates <main-root>/.bee/tmp/bee-herding.enable; running it twice in a row is not an error
`bee herding disable` removes the marker; running it twice in a row (or on an already-absent marker) is not an error
after `bee herding enable`, running .claude/skills/bee-herding/scripts/dispatch-interlock.mjs directly reports enabled:true — the new verb and the existing read-only interlock agree on the same file

artifacts.substantive: exports resolveHerdingMainRoot, enableHerding, disableHerding, herdingStatus; no TODO stubs
covers enable, disable, status --json, idempotency of both enable and disable, and the interlock-agreement truth above

key_links: bee.mjs COMMAND_REGISTRY has herding.enable / herding.disable / herding.status entries
bee.mjs HANDLERS map wires all three to lib/herding.mjs functions
GROUP_USAGE_FALLBACKS has a 'herding' entry calling herdingUsageFallback
prohibitions: no call site under .claude/skills/bee-herding/** or skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/** invokes herding.enable/disable/status programmatically
no TTY/interactivity check added, no exclusion of 'herding' from --help --json (D5)

trace.outcome: Added bee herding enable/disable/status CLI verb group: lib/herding.mjs mirrors dispatch-interlock.mjs's git-common-dir resolution and ENABLE_BASENAME exactly, wired into bee.mjs COMMAND_REGISTRY/HANDLERS/GROUP_USAGE_FALLBACKS. RED-first test_herding_cli.mjs proves idempotent enable/disable and interlock agreement. Deviation: fixed a pre-existing test_bee_cli.mjs drift-guard (group allowlists + example-exercise contract) that the new registry group mechanically broke.
